verses.text: Wisdom has built her house, prepared her feast, and sent her maidens to call from the high places of the city. She makes lavish provision and invites freely. God's wisdom spreads a banquet and summons all to come.

Wisdom has built her house, prepared her feast, and sent her maidens to call from the high places of the city. She makes lavish provision and invites freely. God's wisdom spreads a banquet and summons all to come.

Wisdom has built her house, prepared her feast, and sent her maidens to call from the high places of the city. She makes lavish provision and invites freely. God's wisdom spreads a banquet and summons all to come.

'Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither,' she calls; come, eat my bread, 'forsake the foolish, and live,' and walk in understanding. Her invitation is to life. To accept wisdom's feast is to leave folly and truly live.

'Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither,' she calls; come, eat my bread, 'forsake the foolish, and live,' and walk in understanding. Her invitation is to life. To accept wisdom's feast is to leave folly and truly live.

'Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither,' she calls; come, eat my bread, 'forsake the foolish, and live,' and walk in understanding. Her invitation is to life. To accept wisdom's feast is to leave folly and truly live.

He that reproves a scorner gets shame, but rebuke a wise man and he loves you; 'give instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser.' Correction reveals the heart. The wise grow under reproof; scorners only rage.

He that reproves a scorner gets shame, but rebuke a wise man and he loves you; 'give instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser.' Correction reveals the heart. The wise grow under reproof; scorners only rage.

He that reproves a scorner gets shame, but rebuke a wise man and he loves you; 'give instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser.' Correction reveals the heart. The wise grow under reproof; scorners only rage.

'The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom,' and knowing the Holy is understanding; wisdom lengthens life, and its profit is one's own. Reverence is the foundation again. The fruit of wisdom is gathered first by the one who has it.

'The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom,' and knowing the Holy is understanding; wisdom lengthens life, and its profit is one's own. Reverence is the foundation again. The fruit of wisdom is gathered first by the one who has it.

'The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom,' and knowing the Holy is understanding; wisdom lengthens life, and its profit is one's own. Reverence is the foundation again. The fruit of wisdom is gathered first by the one who has it.
A foolish woman, clamorous and senseless, sits at her door calling to passers-by. Folly also has her invitation. Sin mimics wisdom's call, beckoning the unwary from the same crossroads.
A foolish woman, clamorous and senseless, sits at her door calling to passers-by. Folly also has her invitation. Sin mimics wisdom's call, beckoning the unwary from the same crossroads.
A foolish woman, clamorous and senseless, sits at her door calling to passers-by. Folly also has her invitation. Sin mimics wisdom's call, beckoning the unwary from the same crossroads.

She lures the simple with 'Stolen waters are sweet,' but they know not 'that the dead are there,' her guests in the depths of hell. Her secret pleasures hide death. The feast of folly ends in the grave, however sweet it seems.
